result:
Build is green (only pre-existing warnings, no errors).

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-stdlib/src/string_lib.rs:1691` — the `%p` format handler used `to_pointer(...).unwrap_or(0)` and printed `"0x0"` whenever the argument wasn't a pointer-collectable value. C-Lua instead writes the literal `"(null)"` (per `lstrlib.c:1336-1339`), and also honors the spec's width/`-` flag. The first failing assertion was `assert(string.format("%p", 4) == "(null)")` at strings.lua:165 (combined-file line 174).

**Fix:** Replaced the `b'p'` arm so that when `to_pointer` returns `None` it emits `"(null)"`; when it returns `Some(p)` it emits `"0x{p:x}"`. The handler now also parses the spec between `%` and `p` for the `-` flag and the width digits, and space-pads accordingly (matching tests at strings.lua:176-179).

**State after fix:** Re-tested only the build (per instructions). The `%p` formatting now matches C-Lua for non-pointer arguments and applies width/left-align — the mega-loop should advance past the line-165 assert. I did not re-run the full strings.lua test, since the task instructs not to.
